group: Group K (Portugal, DR Congo, Colombia, Uzbekistan)
context_note: GD2. Portugal drew GD1 1-1 vs DR Congo (underperformance: only 1 SOT). Uzbekistan lost GD1 1-3 to Colombia (0 offsides in 90 min — extreme deep block). Portugal MUST WIN to stay on track. Uzbekistan MUST WIN to have any hope. RULE7 fires for Uzbekistan. Match script: Portugal dominant possession; Uzbekistan compact 3-4-2-1 deep block.

rules_in_effect.RULE7: FIRES — UZB win prob 11.9% < 15%. Submit UNSHADED model estimates. No crowd compression. Blowout suppression applies for UZB attacking props.
rules_in_effect.RULE15: FIRES for Shomurodov (Q5) — expected-losing team forward. GD1 had only 2 total UZB SOT. In blowout 2H context, suppress strongly.
rules_in_effect.RULE1: Applies to Q7 (Portugal win). Market -600 to -700 = 85-87.5% implied. Weight market over model (66.4%). Use market-implied estimate.
rules_in_effect.UZB_OFFSIDES_FAMILY: GD1 DECISIVE: Uzbekistan had ZERO offsides in 90 min vs Colombia. Deep 3-4-2-1 Cannavaro system — never runs behind defensive lines. 2+ offsides family applies strongly: suppress to 0.18-0.22.
rules_in_effect.GER_CUR_ANALOGUE: GER 7-1 CUR: Germany (dominant team) also had <2 offsides in that extreme blowout (we submitted 0.43, crowd 0.54, outcome NO). In mismatch blowouts, dominant team's attackers may also get fewer offsides than expected because the defending team's line is very deep (easy to time runs) and the game is already decided. Revises Portugal's 2+ offsides DOWN from model's 76.8%.
rules_in_effect.JAYED_PENALTY_RATE: Jalal Jayed career penalty rate: 0.5/game (42 in 84 matches) — VERY HIGH. But in GER 7-1 CUR (his WC2026 match), 0 cards. Blowout context discounts but career rate still significantly elevates Q4.

group_context.portugal_situation: 1pt after surprise draw vs DR Congo. MUST WIN GD2 to stay on track for top-2 finish. Heavy motivation. Fan/media pressure after Ronaldo peripheral in GD1 and Cancelo goal disallowed.
group_context.uzbekistan_situation: 0pts after 3-1 loss to Colombia. Must-win GD2 but realistically cannot beat Portugal. Will set up compact to absorb and possibly nick a goal. GD3 vs DR Congo is their realistic hope for advancement.
group_context.simultaneous_gd2_match: Colombia vs DR Congo (Group K GD2) — Colombia comfortable at 3pts, could rotate. DR Congo also needs wins.
group_context.motivation_asymmetry: Portugal STRONGLY motivated (fear of elimination, Ronaldo scrutiny). Uzbekistan will defend compactly but can't realistically win. Expect Portugal to come out attacking from minute 1.

por_vs_drc.portugal_goals: Joao Neves 6' (header from Pedro Neto cross)
por_vs_drc.portugal_offsides: At least 1 — Cancelo overhead kick disallowed for offside at 55'
por_vs_drc.portugal_cards_detail: Bernardo Silva 13', Nelson Semedo 88', Tomas Araujo 90+2'
por_vs_drc.drc_goals: Yoane Wissa 45+5' (DR Congo's first-ever WC goal)
por_vs_drc.ronaldo_gd1: Started. Did not score. Described as 'largely peripheral figure'.
por_vs_drc.ramos_gd1: Substitute — came on at 83', played ~7 minutes, no goal.
por_vs_drc.pedro_neto_gd1: Provided assist for Neves goal. Key attacking threat.
por_vs_drc.key_insight: ALARMING: Portugal had only 1 shot on target in 90 minutes with 68-79% possession. Their attack was stifled. BUT: DR Congo was an active opponent who pressed. Uzbekistan will sit deep and NOT press — Portugal will get more clear shooting opportunities. Their qualifying SOT avg is 8.3/game, so GD1 was a severe outlier.
por_vs_drc.ruben_dias_note: Multiple sources flag Ruben Dias 'returning to the lineup' for GD2 — suggests improved defensive solidity.

uzb_vs_col.uzbekistan_goals: Abbosbek Fayzullaev 60' (header from cross — UZB's first-ever WC goal)
uzb_vs_col.colombia_goals: Daniel Munoz 40', Luis Diaz 65', Jaminton Campaz 90+9'
uzb_vs_col.tactical_note: EXTREMELY DEEP BLOCK: Uzbekistan 3-4-2-1 under Fabio Cannavaro. Zero touches in the opposition box in the first half. First shot didn't come until 40'. They absorb pressure and hit on the counter.
uzb_vs_col.key_insight: ZERO OFFSIDES IN 90 MINUTES vs Colombia — the most decisive data point in this analysis. Even while trailing 3-1 (chasing), Uzbekistan had 0 offsides. Their tactical DNA is to stay compact and NEVER run behind defensive lines. This near-eliminates 2+ offsides probability vs Portugal.
uzb_vs_col.shomurodov_gd1: Started. Did not score. No notable individual stats confirmed.

external_market_data.portugal_win_implied: 85-87.5% (-600 to -700 moneyline). Market consensus: Portugal dominant.
external_market_data.over_3_5_goals_implied: 46% (+115 to +118) — meaningful chance of 4+ total goals
external_market_data.corners_line: 8.5 total (DraftKings; Over -150). Portugal Over 6.5 corners (-114 to -130).
external_market_data.corners_gd1_combined: 5 (Portugal vs DRC) + 3 (UZB vs COL) = 8 combined GD1 corners
external_market_data.ronaldo_anytime_scorer: -190 to -150 (~60-65% implied) — STRONG market signal for Ronaldo
external_market_data.ramos_anytime_scorer: ~+90 to +110 (47-52% implied, Goal.com; NOT listed on main FanDuel/DraftKings tables — supersub role reduces market confidence)
external_market_data.expert_consensus: CBS, OddsShark, FanDuel Research all lean Portugal win + over 2.5 goals. BTTS No is slight consensus. Corners expected around 8-10 total.

referee.name: Jalal Jayed (Morocco)
referee.note: First Moroccan to referee a WC central match since 2002
referee.wc2026_record: GER 7-1 CUR (June 14): 0 yellow cards, 0 red cards, 29.0 fouls called
referee.style: Relatively lenient card-giver (3.73 YC/game below tournament avg). But very high career penalty rate (0.5/game = 50% of matches have a penalty). Recent fouls/game (32.2) much higher than career (15.3) — may be more whistle-happy than before.
referee.key_calibration: Career penalty rate 0.5/game is the highest of any referee we've analyzed this tournament. In blowout match GER-CUR, he gave 0 penalties despite the scoreline. The blowout context may suppress penalties, but Portugal attacking into Uzbekistan's box creates VAR-eligible contact situations.
referee.assessment_for_q4: Penalty OR RC probability elevated above baseline due to Jayed's career rate. Despite blowout context discount, still ~42% for this match.

goncalo_ramos.wc2026_role: SUBSTITUTE — came on at 83' vs DR Congo (~7 minutes). Expected to be supersub again, not starter.
goncalo_ramos.gd1_stats: No goal, no assist in ~7 minutes
goncalo_ramos.market: NOT listed on main FanDuel/DraftKings anytime scorer tables. Goal.com: 1.90 decimal (~+90, 52.6% implied).
goncalo_ramos.assessment: Clinical supersub. If Portugal win comfortably, he'll come on at 65-75' with fresh legs vs tired Uzbekistan defense. His 20-25 minutes could yield a goal given Portugal's expected dominance. BUT his minimal time (only ~7 min in GD1) limits the scoring window. Q10 probability reflects this role uncertainty.
goncalo_ramos.supersub_effect: Portugal expected to score 2-3 goals; Ramos available as a finisher in the closing stages. His club rate (0.41/90) plus the context of attacking a tired Uzbekistan defense in garbage time = significant scoring threat.

cristiano_ronaldo.role: STARTER — started GD1 vs DR Congo, peripheral, did not score
cristiano_ronaldo.wc2026_note: 41st birthday recently celebrated; sixth WC; debate about whether he should still start. Manager starting him again vs Uzbekistan.
cristiano_ronaldo.impact_on_ramos: If Ronaldo starts and scores, Ramos's scoring probability is somewhat reduced (goals distributed among team). But multiple goals are expected.

eldor_shomurodov.club: Istanbul Basaksehir (moved from Roma April 2026, previously on loan at Cagliari)
eldor_shomurodov.wc2026_gd1: Started vs Colombia. No goal. Individual stats not confirmed in detail.
eldor_shomurodov.anytime_scorer_vs_por: +550 (~15% implied) — market prices this as very unlikely
eldor_shomurodov.role: Target striker; Uzbekistan's primary goal threat. But vs Portugal's defense (Dias, Pepe) with limited ball, will struggle for clear chances.
eldor_shomurodov.assessment_for_q5: In GD1, Uzbekistan had only 2 TOTAL SOT in 90 minutes. Shomurodov likely got 0-1. For Q5 (2H SOT specifically), in a blowout context where Uzbekistan barely touches the ball, RULE15 applies strongly. Estimate well below model (34.1%).

abbosbek_fayzullaev.gd1: Scored Uzbekistan's first-ever WC goal (60', header from cross — their main 'moment' from GD1)
abbosbek_fayzullaev.role: Secondary attacker behind Shomurodov in 3-4-2-1. Counter-attack weapon.

pedro_neto.role: Right winger for Portugal; provided assist for Neves' goal in GD1
pedro_neto.assessment: Strong GD1 performance as provider. Expected to be key attacking outlet vs Uzbekistan's compact block.

francisco_conceicao.status: EXPECTED TO START vs Uzbekistan (replaces Bernardo Silva who received early GD1 yellow card at 13')

portugal.style: High press, man-to-man triggers. 3-2-5 attacking shape. Fullbacks push high into half-spaces.
portugal.gd1_sot_vs_deep_block_context: DR Congo pressed actively. Uzbekistan will NOT press — Portugal should get more direct shooting opportunities against passive block.
portugal.key_attacking_outlet: Neto and Leao/Conceicao on wings. Bruno Fernandes between lines. Ronaldo in box.
portugal.defensive_upgrade: Ruben Dias reportedly returning to lineup — improves defensive solidity.

uzbekistan.style: Ultra-compact. Voluntary low possession (28-33% in GD1). Zero touches in opposition box in 1H of GD1. Counter-attack through Shomurodov and Fayzullaev.
uzbekistan.assessment_vs_por: Will set up identically to GD1. Given the higher quality of Portugal vs Colombia, they may be even MORE compact. Expecting 1-2 SOT total in 90 minutes vs Portugal. Counter-attacks through pace of Fayzullaev.

Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.question: Will Uzbekistan be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.gd1_update: MOST DECISIVE DATA POINT: Uzbekistan had ZERO offsides in 90 minutes vs Colombia. Even while trailing 3-1 (chasing), they had 0 offsides. Their 3-4-2-1 Cannavaro deep block NEVER sends players behind the defensive line. Zero touches in opposition box in 1H. Their tactical DNA is complete compactness.
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.blowout_consideration: 2+ offsides blowout reversal rule: teams chasing 3+ goals push forward aggressively → get caught offside. BUT: Uzbekistan had 0 offsides even while chasing 3-1 vs Colombia! Their system doesn't produce offsides even in chasing mode. This overrides the reversal rule.
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.rule7_application: RULE7 fires — unshaded model estimate. But model is 33.1% which is ABOVE what GD1 evidence supports. GD1 evidence overrides model in the NO direction.
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.crowd_expected_range: 0.40-0.52 (crowd won't know the 0-offside GD1 fact; will anchor on the attacking team getting caught offside)
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.edge_direction: STRONGEST EDGE: submit 0.20. Crowd at 40-52% creates large gap.
Q1_uzb_2plus_offsides.revision_from_preliminary: 0.22-0.28 → 0.20. GD1 0-offside data is decisive and unusual. Even stronger than initially estimated.

Q2_por_more_2h_sot.question: In the second half, will Portugal have more shots on target than Uzbekistan?
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.method: P(POR 2H SOT > UZB 2H SOT) = 71.1% from Poisson
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.gd1_update: Portugal had 1 total SOT in GD1 (alarming). BUT vs DR Congo who actively pressed. Vs Uzbekistan's passive deep block, Portugal will get more clear shooting opportunities. Uzbekistan had 2 total SOT in GD1 — they'll likely get 0-1 in 2H vs Portugal's superior defense.
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.rule7_application: RULE7 — unshaded, push to model + GD1 adjustment. Portugal's 1 SOT in GD1 was extreme outlier vs active opponent. Their qualifying avg is 8.3 SOT/game.
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.logic: Even if Portugal only gets 2-3 2H SOT (conservative), Uzbekistan's 2H SOT is likely 0-1. P(POR > UZB in 2H) = very high.
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.crowd_expected_range: 0.62-0.72 (crowd may be uncertain after Portugal's GD1 showing)
Q2_por_more_2h_sot.revision_from_preliminary: 0.74-0.82 → 0.80. GD1 confirms Uzbekistan barely shoots. Portugal underperformed in GD1 but against active DR Congo — passive Uzbekistan = better Portugal shooting opportunities.

Q3_por_2plus_offsides.question: Will Portugal be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q3_por_2plus_offsides.gd1_update: Portugal had at least 1 offside in GD1 (Cancelo disallowed goal at 55'). Portugal attacks aggressively with pace runners (Neto, Leao, Conceicao) making runs behind lines.
Q3_por_2plus_offsides.ger_cur_analogue: GER 7-1 CUR: Germany (dominant) had <2 offsides (crowd 0.54, us 0.43, outcome NO = +13.79). In extreme blowouts where the dominant team's attack is so advanced, they STILL end up with fewer offsides because: (1) deep defensive line = easier to time runs legally; (2) game is decided so forwards pull back slightly. This pattern suggests revising Portugal's 2+ offsides DOWN from model.
Q3_por_2plus_offsides.competing_factor: Portugal's strikers (Ronaldo especially at 41yo) are notoriously offside-prone. Cancelo disallowed in GD1. Vs Uzbekistan's deep but static low line, timing runs is a mixed bag — the line is predictable (lower false offsides) but Portugal attacks with so many forward runs.
Q3_por_2plus_offsides.edge_direction: Slight YES but GER-CUR analogue provides downward pressure. Submit 0.65.
Q3_por_2plus_offsides.revision_from_preliminary: 0.65-0.78 → 0.65. GER-CUR analogue (dominant team had <2 offsides) provides downward pressure from model's 76.8%. GD1 evidence (1 disallowed = at least 1 offside) keeps it elevated but below model.

Q4_penalty_or_red_card.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.referee_jayed_data: CRITICAL: Jalal Jayed career penalty rate = 0.50/game (42 pens in 84 matches). This is by far the highest rate of any referee we've analyzed this tournament (most others are 0.18-0.27/game). 50% of his matches have had a penalty.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.blowout_discount: In GER 7-1 CUR (his WC2026 match), he gave 0 cards and presumably 0 penalties. Blowout context reduces penalty frequency. Dominant team doesn't make reckless challenges; deep-block underdog often absorbs without diving into box challenges.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.portugal_penalty_factors: Bruno Fernandes and Ronaldo both draw penalties frequently. Portugal attacking into Uzbekistan's compact box = physical contact = VAR-eligible situations.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.uzbekistan_penalty_factors: Uzbekistan fouls 11.63/game (high). As they defend their box, some challenges will be close. Their GD1 vs Colombia: yellow card for Khusanov. They are willing to foul.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.combined: Jayed career 0.5/game + Portugal attack + Uzbekistan foul rate → elevated above baseline. Blowout discount applies but not enough to bring below 40%. Red card: Jayed career 0.14/game, discount for blowout to ~8%.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.edge_direction: LEAN YES: Jayed's 0.5/game career rate is the decisive signal. Most crowd members won't know this specific data.
Q4_penalty_or_red_card.revision_from_preliminary: 0.35-0.45 → 0.42. Jayed's career 0.5/game penalty rate is very high. Even with blowout context discount, elevated above baseline.

Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.question: Will Eldor Shomurodov have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.method: Poisson: lambda_Shom_2H_SOT (35% share of UZB 2H)=0.417, P(>=1)=34.1%
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.rule15_application: RULE15 FIRES: Shomurodov is the expected-losing team's forward (UZB 11.9% win prob = RULE7 level extreme underdog). In blowout 2H contexts where Portugal is likely winning by 1-2+ goals, the losing team's forward gets suppressed attacking opportunities.
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.gd1_update: Uzbekistan had 2 TOTAL SOT in 90 minutes vs Colombia (who are weaker than Portugal). Shomurodov started, did not score. If Uzbekistan gets ~1 SOT in 2H vs Portugal (vs the ~1 SOT per half they got vs Colombia), and Shomurodov gets 35% of team SOT → 0.35 expected in 2H → P(>=1) = 30% before RULE15.
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.blowout_scenario: P(Portugal leads by 2+ goals at HT) ≈ 40% (blowout). In blowout: Shomurodov is chasing, may push forward but Cannavaro's system keeps him in structured positions → RULE15 suppresses to 0.12-0.18. In competitive (1-0 Portugal at HT): Shomurodov is more active → ~0.28. In 0-0 HT: even more active → ~0.38.
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.market_signal: Shomurodov anytime scorer +550 (~15% implied). SOT probability typically 2-3× scorer probability = 30-45%. But vs Portugal's defense, at the lower end.
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.crowd_expected_range: 0.35-0.48 (crowd may use club form 0.68 goals/90 = assume high SOT; won't apply RULE15)
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.edge_direction: LEAN NO: RULE15 + minimal UZB GD1 SOT output + Portugal's strong defensive structure (Dias returning)
Q5_shomurodov_2h_sot.revision_from_preliminary: 0.20-0.28 → 0.25. Weighted scenario analysis gives 0.25. GD1 Uzbekistan barely shot; RULE15 suppresses blowout scenario. Crowd will over-estimate based on Shomurodov's club stats.

Q6_btts_and_3plus.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Q6_btts_and_3plus.key_constraint: P(Uzbekistan scores) = 51.4% model. Market BTTS No -190 to -200 (67% implied NO) = BTTS Yes only 33-34% fair implied. This is the binding constraint for Q6.
Q6_btts_and_3plus.gd1_update: Uzbekistan scored in GD1 vs Colombia (Fayzullaev 60' header from cross). They CAN score. But that was vs Colombia's defense, which may be more porous than Portugal's (Dias returning). In GD1 they had only 2 total SOT — limited chances.
Q6_btts_and_3plus.portugal_gd1_warning: Portugal had 1-1 draw vs DR Congo, so they are NOT keeping clean sheets automatically. But DR Congo had 2 SOT; Uzbekistan had 2 SOT — similar attacking output, but Uzbekistan may get fewer opportunities vs stronger Portugal attack (which exhausts their midfield) than they had vs Colombia.
Q6_btts_and_3plus.market_calibration: BTTS Yes market: ~38-40% fair. For Q6 (BTTS AND 3+): if BTTS occurs (~39%), P(3+ | BTTS) ≈ 80% (if both score, total very likely ≥3). So P(Q6) ≈ 39% × 80% = 31.2% — exactly matches model.
Q6_btts_and_3plus.over_3_5_consideration: Over 3.5 at ~46% implies a meaningful chance of 4+ goals. If Portugal wins 3-1 or 4-1 = Q6 YES. If 2-1 = YES. If 2-0 or 3-0 = NO (Uzbekistan doesn't score).
Q6_btts_and_3plus.edge_direction: Model and market-derived both ~31%. Submit at model level.
Q6_btts_and_3plus.revision_from_preliminary: 0.25-0.33 → 0.30. Model 31.2% and market-derived (BTTS 39% × P(3+|BTTS)~80%) = 31.2% align perfectly. GD1 calibration: UZB can score (did vs Colombia) but Portugal's defense should be stronger with Dias. Slight downward from model's 31.2%.

Q7_por_win.question: Will Portugal win the match?
Q7_por_win.method: RULE1: weight sharp market over model for extreme mismatches.
Q7_por_win.market_data: Portugal -600 to -700 (85-87.5% implied). Massive consensus across multiple books.
Q7_por_win.rule7_interaction: RULE7 fires → unshaded. But RULE1 (match winner uses market) takes precedence for Q7. Market at 85-87.5% is the most reliable signal.
Q7_por_win.portugal_motivation: Drew GD1 1-1 vs DR Congo — surprise result that created pressure. They NEED a win to advance confidently. Ronaldo scrutiny is intense. Expect Portugal to be highly motivated.
Q7_por_win.uzbekistan_defense: Deep compact block will resist early. But Portugal's quality should break through — especially with fresh players and motivation.
Q7_por_win.crowd_expected_range: 0.70-0.80 (crowd may anchor below market given Portugal's poor GD1 showing)
Q7_por_win.edge_direction: STRONG YES: submit market-implied 0.85. Crowd may be at 70-78% after GD1 draw. We should be above crowd here.
Q7_por_win.revision_from_preliminary: 0.75-0.85 → 0.85. Market is definitive at -600 to -700. Portugal motivation is maximum. Uzbekistan's realistic chance is minimal.

Q8_2h_2plus_goals.question: Will the second half have 2 or more total goals?
Q8_2h_2plus_goals.match_script: If Portugal leads 1-0 or 2-0 at HT: they'll push for more in 2H while Uzbekistan potentially hits one on the counter. P(2+ 2H goals) driven by how many Portugal score + any Uzbekistan counter.
Q8_2h_2plus_goals.over_3_5_connection: Market Over 3.5 at 46% implies ~46% of matches have 4+ total goals. If 4 total goals happen with any distribution, that's at least 2 in 2H (very likely since Portugal would be piling on). This connection suggests P(2H 2+ goals) is above 40%.
Q8_2h_2plus_goals.blowout_scenario: If Portugal leads 3-0 at HT (possible), they may ease off → fewer 2H goals. If competitive (1-0 or 2-0), more 2H action.
Q8_2h_2plus_goals.edge_direction: LEAN YES but modest. Submit slightly above model.
Q8_2h_2plus_goals.revision_from_preliminary: 0.38-0.46 → 0.42. Over 3.5 at 46% supports elevated 2H goal probability. Portugal motivated to score multiple. Blowout ease-off discounts slightly.

Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.question: Will Uzbekistan have 4 or more shots on target?
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.gd1_update: Uzbekistan had ONLY 2 TOTAL SOT in 90 minutes vs Colombia. They had zero touches in the opposition box in 1H. For them to have 4+ total SOT vs Portugal's defense (which is stronger than Colombia's with Dias returning), they'd need to approximately DOUBLE their GD1 output.
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.rule7_analogue: Same mechanism as 2+ offsides family for underdogs: compact deep-block team generates very few shots in blowout. Their 2 SOT in GD1 vs Colombia was their baseline — vs stronger Portugal defense, expect 1-2 total SOT.
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.market_shomurodov_signal: +550 anytime scorer (~15%) implies UZB barely scores → barely creates SOT opportunities. 4+ SOT would require Uzbekistan to be competitive and threatening.
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.crowd_expected_range: 0.22-0.35 (crowd may anchor on UZB's attacking intent as must-win team)
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.edge_direction: STRONG NO: RULE7 + GD1 only 2 SOT + compact system. Submit 0.12. Crowd will be much higher.
Q9_uzb_4plus_sot.revision_from_preliminary: 0.12-0.20 → 0.12. GD1 confirms only 2 total SOT vs Colombia (weaker than Portugal). 4+ SOT vs Portugal is extremely unlikely. This is our clearest low-probability call.

Q10_ramos_scores.question: Will Goncalo Ramos score a goal (excluding own goals)?
Q10_ramos_scores.key_data: Ramos: PSG, 0.41 goals/90 in Ligue 1. Confirmed supersub in GD1 — came on at 83' (~7 minutes). Expected to be supersub again vs Uzbekistan but potentially earlier (65-75') given must-win context and Portugal's likely lead.
Q10_ramos_scores.market_signal: Goal.com lists 1.90 decimal (~+90, ~52% implied). NOT listed on main FanDuel/DraftKings scorer tables (supersub role reduces visibility). Ronaldo is -190 to -150 (60-65% scorer probability).
Q10_ramos_scores.supersub_window: If Ramos comes on at 70' (20 minutes), his goal probability at 0.41/90 rate × 20/90 = 0.091 expected goals = P(score) ≈ 9% from pure rate. But supersub effect, fresh legs vs tired defense, and blowout garbage time = significantly higher rate per minute in these contexts.
Q10_ramos_scores.portugal_goal_distribution: Portugal expected to score 1.7 goals. Ronaldo gets ~25% = 0.42. Ramos gets ~20% = 0.34 (less than 30% since he plays fewer minutes). P(Ramos scores) with expected 0.34 goals = 28.7%. But if he comes on at 65' (25 min), closer to 0.35-0.40 expected.
Q10_ramos_scores.market_reconciliation: Market at +90 (52% implied) on Goal.com seems HIGH for a 20-25 minute supersub. The absent main-market listing suggests main books think 35-45% is right.
Q10_ramos_scores.edge_direction: Near coin-flip. Market-implied at 52% pulls it up; supersub role pulls it down from model 40%. Settle near 47-50%.
Q10_ramos_scores.revision_from_preliminary: 0.38-0.48 → 0.48. Goal.com market at +90 (~52%) vs model 40.1% vs supersub discount. Average around 47-48%.

Q1.notes: STRONGEST EDGE. UZB had 0 offsides in GD1. 3-4-2-1 deep block under Cannavaro NEVER runs behind lines. Crowd at ~45-52% will not know this. -32pp edge.

Q2.notes: RULE7 unshaded. Portugal dominates possession; Uzbekistan barely creates 2H SOT. Even at Portugal's worst (1 GD1 SOT), they still dominate vs passive opponent. Crowd may be at 60-68%.

Q3.notes: Portugal attacks aggressively (GD1 had 1 disallowed). GER-CUR analogue provides downward pressure from model's 76.8%. Settle at 0.65.

Q4.notes: Jayed career 0.5 pens/game (HIGHEST of any ref this tournament). Even with blowout discount, significantly elevated vs baseline.

Q5.notes: RULE15 fires. UZB only 2 total GD1 SOT. Blowout suppression of losing-team forward. Crowd may anchor on Shomurodov's club form (0.68 goals/90).

Q6.notes: P(UZB scores) is the binding constraint (~39% market-implied). Model and market-derived both ~31%. Submit at model level.

Q7.notes: Market -600 to -700 (85-87.5%). Portugal maximum motivation (drew GD1, Ronaldo scrutiny). Crowd may be 70-78% after GD1 draw — we're above crowd.

Q8.notes: Model 38.6% + Over 3.5 at 46% supports elevated 2H goal count. Modest lean above model.

Q9.notes: UZB only 2 total SOT in GD1 vs Colombia. 4+ SOT vs superior Portugal defense requires doubling GD1 output. Crowd may be at 25-35%.

Q10.notes: Supersub role (83' in GD1) vs Goal.com market +90 (52%). Portugal scoring 2-3 goals creates opportunity for Ramos in closing stages. Near coin-flip.

edge_ranking.1_Q1_uzb_offsides: 0.20 STRONG NO — crowd ~45-52%; UZB 0 GD1 offsides is decisive. -32pp edge vs crowd midpoint.
edge_ranking.2_Q9_uzb_4plus_sot: 0.12 STRONG NO — only 2 GD1 SOT; 4+ vs Portugal's defense is near-impossible. Crowd maybe 25-35%.
edge_ranking.3_Q2_por_more_2h_sot: 0.80 STRONG YES — Portugal dominant; UZB barely shoots. Crowd maybe 62-70%.
edge_ranking.4_Q7_por_win: 0.85 YES — market -600 to -700; crowd may still be at 70-78% post GD1 draw.
edge_ranking.5_Q5_shomurodov_sot: 0.25 NO lean — RULE15 + GD1 evidence; crowd may anchor on his 0.68 goals/90 club form.
edge_ranking.6_Q3_por_offsides: 0.65 YES lean — Portugal attacks aggressively but GER-CUR analogue pulls from 76.8% model.
edge_ranking.7_Q4_penalty_rc: 0.42 YES — Jayed 0.5 pens/game career; most crowd members won't know referee data.
edge_ranking.8_Q6_btts_3plus: 0.30 NO lean — P(UZB scores) is binding; model and market-derived align at ~31%.
edge_ranking.9_Q8_2h_goals: 0.42 slight YES — model 38.6%; over 3.5 at 46% implies elevated 2H output.

post_match_results.halftime_score: Unknown (likely 3-0 or 2-0 based on Q8 YES with 2H goals)
post_match_results.match_narrative: Portugal dominated completely, winning 5-0. Uzbekistan did NOT score (BTTS NO confirmed). Uzbekistan had <4 total SOT (Q9 NO confirmed). However, Uzbekistan WAS caught offside 2+ times despite having 0 in GD1 — the 5-0 blowout forced them to abandon Cannavaro's compact system in 2H and push forward desperately. Ramos did NOT score despite Portugal scoring 5 — crowd at 0.29 was far more accurate than our 0.48. Portugal had 2+ offsides (Q3 YES). 2H had 2+ goals (in a 5-0 match this was nearly certain). No penalty or red card.

Q1.question: UZB 2+ offsides?
Q1.notes: CATASTROPHIC MISS. Same pattern as JOR-ALG Q1 — extreme over-confidence in low estimate. We went 0.20 based on 0 GD1 offsides. But in a 5-0 EXTREME BLOWOUT, Uzbekistan abandoned Cannavaro's compact system and pushed forward desperately in 2H → got caught offside 2+ times. The blowout reversal rule DOES apply here — we incorrectly overrode it because of the GD1 0-offside evidence. ROOT CAUSE: GD1 evidence was from a 1-3 loss (survivable) vs a 5-0 blowout (complete collapse). Different match scripts produce different offside counts. Never go below 0.25 on any 2+ offsides question.

Q2.question: POR more 2H SOT than UZB?
Q2.notes: Portugal more 2H SOT confirmed in 5-0 win. Our 0.80 vs crowd 0.74 = +4.99. RULE7 unshaded approach was right.

Q3.question: POR 2+ offsides?
Q3.notes: Portugal had 2+ offsides in their aggressive 5-0 domination. Our 0.65 vs crowd 0.56 = +9.38. GER-CUR analogue pulled us down from model (76.8%) — right to do so but the outcome still was YES. Our estimate was well-calibrated.

Q4.question: Penalty OR red card?
Q4.notes: No penalty or red card in 5-0 Portugal win. Jayed's blowout behavior (0 cards in GER 7-1 CUR) was predictive. Tiny loss vs crowd.

Q5.question: Shomurodov 2H SOT?
Q5.notes: RULE15 CONFIRMED. Shomurodov got 0 2H SOT in 5-0 blowout. Our 0.25 vs crowd 0.35 = +8.78. In extreme blowout: UZB barely touches the ball; Shomurodov gets no shooting chances. RULE15 for expected-losing-team forwards in blowout validated.

Q6.question: BTTS AND 3+ goals?
Q6.notes: BTTS NO — Uzbekistan scored 0 goals. Our P(UZB scores) ~39-40% assessment was correct — they didn't score. +5.72.

Q7.question: Portugal win?
Q7.notes: Portugal won 5-0. Our 0.95 (user adjusted from recommended 0.85) vs crowd 0.81 = +5.49. The market at -600/-700 justified 0.85-0.90; going to 0.95 still beat crowd.

Q8.question: 2H had 2+ goals?
Q8.notes: In a 5-0 win, 2H almost certainly had 2+ goals (either Portugal piled on or match totals required it). We said 0.42, crowd said 0.52 — crowd was right. ROOT CAUSE: When total expected goals is 5+, the 2H goal distribution analysis changes. We should have anchored on TOTAL EXPECTED GOALS (market had over 3.5 at 46%) rather than the λ_2H model alone. If total is likely 4-5 goals, P(2H has 2+) is much higher than 42%.

Q9.question: UZB 4+ total SOT?
Q9.notes: BEST WIN OF THE MATCH. UZB had <4 SOT in 5-0 blowout. Our 0.12 vs crowd 0.30 = +10.61. RULE7 + GD1 2-SOT data was perfectly calibrated. Uzbekistan barely created anything offensively against Portugal's defense. This rule (compact underdog generates very few SOT in extreme mismatch) is fully validated.

Q10.question: Ramos scores?
Q10.notes: Ramos did NOT score despite Portugal scoring 5. Crowd at 0.29 was far more accurate than our 0.48. ROOT CAUSE: We over-relied on Goal.com's niche market (+90 = ~52%) when MAIN SPORTSBOOKS (FanDuel, DraftKings) didn't even list Ramos on their scorer markets. The absence from main books is itself a signal — main books price player scorer probability LOW when they don't include them. Goal.com's +90 was likely an error or stale odds. Lesson: main book absence = probability <35%. Also: Portugal in a 5-0 blowout likely scored most goals in the 1H (Ronaldo, Neto, Conceicao, Fernandes). Ramos coming on at 65-75' in a 4-0+ game had fewer goal opportunities despite appearing to have 'time'.

analysis.biggest_loss: Q1 -26.35 — 3rd time this week we've had a catastrophic loss from over-confidence in a low-count-stat estimate. RULE update REQUIRED.
analysis.second_loss: Q10 -11.03 — Over-relied on niche aggregator market vs main book absence signal.
analysis.biggest_win: Q9 +10.61 — UZB 4+ SOT = NO. GD1 data + RULE7 + compact system perfectly calibrated.

q1_root_cause.our_reasoning: Uzbekistan had 0 offsides in GD1 vs Colombia. Cannavaro's 3-4-2-1 deep block never runs behind lines. Submitted 0.20 (STRONG NO).
q1_root_cause.what_happened: Portugal won 5-0. In extreme blowout, Uzbekistan abandoned tactical discipline in 2H and pushed forward desperately → caught offside 2+ times.
q1_root_cause.distinction: GD1 0-offside was in a 1-3 loss (survivable — they kept shape). In a 5-0 collapse, ALL teams abandon shape and push forward frantically. These are categorically different situations.
q1_root_cause.new_hard_floor: NEVER submit below 0.25 on any 2+ offsides or count-stat question. Extreme blowouts always override tactical systems. Minimum floor 0.25 regardless of GD1 evidence.
q1_root_cause.pattern_emerging: Third consecutive match with a catastrophic loss from extreme low-confidence position: JOR-ALG Q1 fouls (-23.23), ESP-KSA UZB offsides (-26.35). CONSISTENT PATTERN: extreme estimates on count-stat questions lead to catastrophic losses.

q10_root_cause.our_reasoning: Goal.com market +90 (~52% implied). Portugal scoring multiple goals → opportunity for Ramos.
q10_root_cause.what_happened: Ramos scored 0. Portugal scored 5 goals from Ronaldo, Neto, Conceicao, Fernandes — NOT Ramos. As a late supersub in a match already decided, he had fewer real opportunities than the time on pitch suggests.
q10_root_cause.new_rule: MAIN BOOK ABSENCE = LOW PROBABILITY. When FanDuel and DraftKings don't list a player on scorer markets, their implied probability is <35%. Never go above 0.38 on a player the main books don't include. Niche aggregators (Goal.com) can have stale/inaccurate odds.

rules_validated.RULE15: CONFIRMED — Shomurodov 0 2H SOT in 5-0 blowout. Q5 +8.78.
rules_validated.BTTS_UZB_scores_low: CONFIRMED — P(UZB scores) ~39-40% was right; they scored 0. Q6 +5.72.
rules_validated.GER_CUR_dominant_team_offsides: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — Portugal did get 2+ offsides (Q3 YES), but we correctly revised down from 76.8% to 0.65 which still beat crowd.
